TextENGNew: I let out an enormous yawn.
I felt like I had only just managed to fall asleep when morning arrived...
Mom set out breakfast with a wry smile.
``What's the matter, Keiichi?@k@vS02/00/132700002. What time did you go to bed last night?``
``I went to bed around midnight, but... I just couldn't fall asleep for some reason...``
``You probably got too excited at the festival and couldn't calm down.``
``Th-That's not it...! Crunch crunch!``
I stuffed my mouth with pickled radish and crunched down hard to hide my embarrassment.
changeReason: New adds 'stuffed my mouth with' (頬張る = stuff one's cheeks), a detail present in the Japanese that old omitted.

TextENGNew: ...At the very end of last night, there was the sound that Shion told me about.
changeReason: Old says 'After all that happened yesterday'; new says 'At the very end of last night,' matching 昨夜の最後の最後で.

TextENGNew: ...That banging noise, like a child jumping up and down on a wooden floor somewhere far away.
The story clung unpleasantly to my mind, making it impossible to sleep...
It was a long night...@k I jumped at every faint rustle of distant treetops—sounds that should have been either inaudible or too insignificant to notice.
Sitting here now in the morning sunlight, listening to the birds sing, I could see how cowardly that had been, but...
...It was a morning where I felt anything but refreshed.
``Keiichi-kuuun! Good mooorning!``
``Huh?@k@vS02/02/130200073. Keiichi-kun, your eyes are all red!@k@vS02/02/130200074. I wonder why? Why?``
``Oh, hey, morning.@k@vS02/01/130100209. For some reason I couldn't get to sleep last night.@k@vS02/01/130100210. So I'm running on no sleep today.@k@vS02/01/130100211. ...*Yaaawn*``
``The festival yesterday was so much fun...!@k@vS02/02/130200076. Rena was so excited she couldn't sleep either! Couldn't sleep!``
In Rena's case, it seemed like her excitement still hadn't worn off...
``I wish we could have festivals like that more often!``
``So everyone can have a blast together again. Hau!``
``Mm, yeah... Sure.``
Rena bringing up yesterday's festival made all the memories from last night come flooding back.
...The inside of the ritual storehouse had been a world apart from the peaceful, sunny everyday life of Hinamizawa.
And then there were all those stories Takano-san had told me at length...
The mysterious incidents that occurred every year on the day of the Watanagashi Festival.
The bizarre pattern where one person would die and one would vanish.
...Behind them all, the old legends of Hinamizawa flickered like shadows.@k The numerous, horrifying customs of Onigafuchi Village.
Villagers with the blood of demons flowing through their veins.
The terrible feasts of human flesh.
The 'spiriting away'—the Onikakushi—where wretched sacrifices were snatched from human settlements.
changeReason: Old structures the sentence differently, placing 'victims of the Onikakushi' as a separate fragment; new integrates 'spiriting away' as a direct gloss of Onikakushi, matching how the Japanese explains the term.

TextENGNew: And to appease the wrath of Oyashiro-sama... the ritual of living sacrifice.
......And more, and more.@k ...Just recalling them was enough to sink me into a dark mood.
And most of them weren't fiction—they were fact.
...But there was no need to be afraid just because they were real.@k ...It was all in the distant past, after all.@k Yes. The distant past.
``Mii-chan's late, isn't she?``
Before I knew it, we had arrived at our usual meeting spot with Mion.
...I must have been completely lost in thought.
``We have a rule that if she's not here by now, we can go on ahead, right?@k@vS02/02/130200081. ...What should we do?``
It wasn't that unusual for Mion to be late, but... today she was later than ever.
......Several of last night's ominous conversations came flooding back into my mind.
Who would die this year, and who would disappear...
......S-Surely not...... Mion...?
``What do you want to do, Keiichi-kun?@k@vS02/02/130200083. ...If we don't leave soon, we'll be late.``
``......She'll come.@k@vS02/01/130100215. She didn't call to say she'd be absent, did she?@k@vS02/01/130100216. Let's wait five more minutes. Okay?``
Rena glanced at her watch with a slightly troubled expression, but then immediately nodded with a bright smile.
Hmm...
If we waited five minutes, we'd have to sprint to school to make it on time...
Just as I thought that, I spotted Mion running toward us, gasping for breath.
``Mii-chaaaaan!!@k@vS02/02/130200085. You're sooo laaate!!``
``Hah!@k@vS02/03/130300081. Hah!@k@vS02/03/130300082. ...You waited for me?@k@vS02/03/130300083. Y-You could've just gone ahead without me...``
...Mion's face was flushed red, and she was panting heavily.
...Her house was just up the road from here.@k It shouldn't have been far enough to leave her this winded.
``......Mii-chan... Are you maybe not feeling well?``
Huh?@k ...Now that she mentioned it...
...Rena brushed Mion's bangs aside and pressed her hand against her forehead.
``...Your hand's cold, Rena.``
``Mii-chan... you have a bit of a fever, you know?``
``It's nothing serious.@k@vS02/03/130300086. ...Give me half a day and I'll be right as rain.``
``Are you really okay?@k@vS02/01/130100218. ...Don't push yourself too hard, all right?``
``Oh?@k@vS02/03/130300088. Kei-chan, are you worried about me?@k@vS02/03/130300089. Ahaha... that makes me happy.``
...That wasn't like Mion at all.
...She must have been feeling really bad.
``I-I really think you shouldn't push yourself.@k@vS02/02/130200089. We can tell the teacher you're taking the day off.``
``I'm fine, I'm fine!@k@vS02/03/130300091. I already took some cold medicine.@k@vS02/03/130300092. It should kick in any minute now.``
Mion forced an energetic expression and flexed her bicep at us.
``Then... let's get going.@k@vS02/01/130100220. Take it easy, all right?—is what I'd say, but...@k@vS02/01/130100221. ...the time.``
``Wah?!@k@vS02/02/130200091. W-We have to run or we w-won't make it~!!``
``Ah-hahahahaha!@k@vS02/03/130300094. What a thrilling start to the morning!@k@vS02/03/130300095. Maybe this is just the jolt my sluggish body needs!@k@vS02/03/130300096. Let's go!``
Hey, hey... you're sick, don't overdo it...!
But Mion had already taken off running with a show of energy.
``...Mii-chan is the oldest among us, you know?@k@vS02/02/130200093. She knows her own body better than anyone.``
If Mion said she was fine, I'd take her word for it.
``All right, let's go too!!``
We broke into a run after Mion...
The school looked the same as always.
The languid hours were brutal on someone running on no sleep...
If there were an Olympic event for sleeping soundly, I'd be a gold medalist right now.
The trick in dangerous situations like this is to hold your mechanical pencil upside down.
...Why, you ask?
So you don't scrawl strange doodles all over your notes while half-asleep.
If you're holding the pencil upside down, that won't happen, right?
Bonk bonk bonk! Bonk bonk bonk!
Something like an eraser struck me on the head six times.
``Ow, ow, ow... What are you doing, Satoko?!``
``I-I haven't done a single thing...!``
``Who else would pull a prank like this?!@k@vS02/01/130100225. Oh, wait—this isn't an eraser, it's chalk.@k@vS02/01/130100226. ......Huh?``
Behind me... looming like a female delinquent gripping razor blades, was the teacher's shadow, three pieces of chalk clutched in each hand...@k Rumble rumble rumble......
changeReason: Old says 'female outlaw'; new says 'female delinquent' — both translate スケ番 but 'delinquent' (sukeban) is the standard translation for this Japanese subculture term.

TextENGNew: ``Maebara-kun.@k@vS02/22/131400002. ...Go wash your face.``
``Uh...... R-Right, g-gladly...!``
That spectacle alone had been enough to jolt me awake... but the teacher's sheer intimidation sent me flying out into the hallway.
If I was going to wash my face anyway, I'd rather use the outdoor sink than the one in the dim utility room.@k ...With that thought, I headed out through the entrance.
The sunlight was intense.@k ...Hard to believe it was only June.
I turned the faucet, and with a pathetic gurgling sound, lukewarm water came sputtering out.
...If I let it run for a while, would it turn cold?
...I stood there absent-mindedly, lost in thought.
``I figured you'd pick these sinks, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/03/130300098. It just feels better out front, doesn't it?``
``Hm... Mion?@k@vS02/01/130100229. ...*Yaaawn*@k@vS02/01/130100230. Hey, no cutting class now~``
I held my hand under the stream. The water had turned so cold it was hard to believe it had ever been warm.
I cupped it in my hands and splashed it against my face.
I moved aside, and Mion did the same—splashing water over her face.
``...Mion, how's your cold?@k@vS02/01/130100232. You're not sleepy from lack of sleep—it's the medicine's side effects, right?``
``Hm?@k@vS02/03/130300100. ...Ahahahaha. Actually, don't tell anyone, but... after the festival yesterday, I got roped into a family gathering.``
``And, well... it kind of turned into an all-nighter.``
An all-nighter.
So in other words... you don't have a cold at all, you're just...
changeReason: Old omits key content: Japanese says 日本語に直すと連続 (in Japanese it means 'continuous/nonstop'); old just says 'So you mean to say...' while new renders 'So in other words...' Both miss the word play but new at least transitions the logic better.

TextENGNew: ``So you really are just sleep-deprived?!@k@vS02/01/130100234. If you pulled an all-nighter, just say so! That was misleading...``
``Nahahaha...!@k@vS02/03/130300103. Give me a break.@k@vS02/03/130300104. I really do feel awful, you know!``
...I suppose feeling lousy was the same either way.@k Still, you treat a cold and sleep deprivation very differently.
``And here I was, worried for nothing.@k@vS02/01/130100236. Geez... that's so like you, Mion...``
Mion scratched her head sheepishly.
...She certainly didn't look well, but knowing it was just sleep deprivation put my mind at ease. A little rest and she'd bounce right back.
``Oh, but the pounding headache is real.@k@vS02/03/130300106. The teacher actually told me to go home early.``
``Hey, no fair...!@k@vS02/01/130100238. So you're heading home now, Mion?``
Now that I looked, Mion had her bag with her. She was clearly on her way out.

``Yeah. Sorry about that.@k@vS02/03/130300108. I'll be a good girl and rest at home today.@k@vS02/03/130300109. ...Sorry I can't make it to club activities.@k@vS02/03/130300110. Kei-chan, could you take over as leader for today?``

``......I doubt that'll happen.@k@vS02/01/130100240. We have that unspoken rule that we don't hold club meetings unless everyone's there, right?@k@vS02/01/130100241. Whenever you had to go to your part-time job, we always just called it off.``
Mion laughed wryly, saying we didn't need to worry about her.
But no matter how much fun we might have, feeling bad about a friend who couldn't share in it—that's the mark of a truly wonderful friendship.
In that sense, the club Mion had built was something really special.
While I was thinking, the water on my face from washing had completely dried.
...Come to think of it, we'd been chatting too long.@k I should get back to the classroom before I got in trouble.
``Well, see you.@k@vS02/01/130100243. I'm heading back to class.@k@vS02/01/130100244. Be careful going home.@k@vS02/01/130100245. Don't go wobbling into a ditch or a drainage canal, all right?``
With that, I turned on my heel to head back to the classroom.
``Oh... that's right, Kei-chan.``
Mion called out to stop me, so I turned my head back to listen.
``What is it?``
``This might sound strange, but just answer without thinking too hard about it.``
``Well, that depends on what it is.``
``Last night, toward the end of the festival... did you happen to run into Tomitake-san or Takano-san?``
......I froze, my body going rigid.
``You know Tomitake-san and Takano-san, right?@k@vS02/03/130300115. Remember, Kei-chan? The day before the festival, during setup, the four of you were chatting together with Shion.``
If I could, I wanted to forget about sneaking into the storehouse that night as quickly as possible.
...So hearing the names of the people who had been there with me was deeply unsettling.
...More importantly, though.
...Why was Mion... asking me something like that...?
Unable to answer right away, I found myself staring into Mion's eyes...
...Was Mion's question—'did you see them?'—a genuine question, or an interrogation from someone who already knew the answer...? I couldn't tell.
``......W-Well... I'm not sure.@k@vS02/01/130100249. ...I might have seen them, I might not have...``
My evasive answer was clearly displeasing to Mion.
...What answer had she been hoping to hear from me...? There was no way to know.
``............I see.@k@vS02/03/130300117. Then one more question.@k@vS02/03/130300118. Same night.``
``Did you see Shion?``
My heart lurched again.@k ......A tiny needle, hidden beneath Mion's words, pricked into me.
``...Didn't... didn't you ask me that yesterday?@k@vS02/01/130100251. ...I thought I told you I wasn't sure...``
``Hm, did you?@k@|@y@vS02/03/130300121. ...I just thought I might get a different answer if I asked again.``
In Mion's gaze... something indescribable had taken root without my noticing.
Tomitake-san and Takano-san.
And Shion.
...And she was asking me about all of them.
......There was no mistake.
...Last night, the four of us who snuck into the ritual storehouse... every single one.
...B-But... so what?!@k It wasn't as if there was really a curse or anything......!
...That's right.
Both Shion and Takano-san had said before the festival that there was no curse.
...That rather than a curse, it was the work of the villagers...@k ..................................My blood ran cold.
...Mion... almost certainly... knew that we had snuck into the storehouse...?
That shouldn't have been surprising.
......Last night, I had made the fatal mistake of flipping on the light switch.
...If Mion had been looking for me, it was easy to imagine she might have spotted that momentary flash of light and come to investigate.
``...Did you really not see her?``
``Wh-Why does it matter so much whether I saw them or not?``

``It's nothing important.@k@vS02/03/130300124. ...It's just that some people...@k@vS02/03/130300125. ...have been saying bad things about Tomitake-san, Takano-san, Shion, and you, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/03/130300126. ......I didn't catch what exactly they were saying, though.``
A cold sweat crept down my arms... and the sound of it dripping from my fingertips was maddeningly loud...
``I don't believe you did anything wrong, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/03/130300128. ...I just wanted to ask, so I did.@k@vS02/03/130300129. ...Sorry if I made you uncomfortable.``
......I couldn't bring myself to say a word.
...My reaction must have been telling the truth for me, plain as day... yet I couldn't compose myself.
``Kei-chan, you weren't with Tomitake-san, Takano-san, or Shion.@k@vS02/03/130300131. ......Right?``
I finally wrung out an answer between labored breaths. Mion's expression softened slightly.
``......Good.@k@vS02/03/130300133. I'll tell them, then.@k@vS02/03/130300134. That you weren't involved in anything bad, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/03/130300135. ...I'll make sure everyone knows.``
...Yeah... please do.
...Tell them I didn't... do anything wrong.
...To everyone... make sure...@k ......Everyone.@k ......Everyone... who is 'everyone'...?
``See you later.@k@vS02/03/130300137. I'll be all better by tomorrow!@k@vS02/03/130300138. Bye-bye!``
W-Wait, Mion!!@k Who do you mean by 'everyone'...?!!
At that instant!!@k An ice-cold hand pressed against the back of my neck!!!!
``Hey!@k@vS02/22/131400004. You're taking too long, Maebara-kun.@k@vS02/22/131400005. How long does it take to wash your face?!``
......It was the teacher.
...I had been gone so long she came out to fetch me...
``Oh, sorry...@k@vS02/01/130100255. I was chatting with Mion and lost track of time......``
I turned around, but Mion had already crossed the schoolyard and was standing at the gate.
``Now then, back to your studies!@k@vS02/22/131400007. Hurry, hurry.``
Urged on by the teacher, I headed back toward the entrance.
...When I looked back one more time, Mion was already out of sight.
``......Keiichi, you've been down all day.``
``It's obviously because he went wild at the festival yesterday!``
``I don't want to hear that from the girl who got lost and was practically in tears with her face all red.``
``Ah, ahahahaha~!!``
``S-Satoko-chan last night was so, so cuuute! Hau~!!``
My friends were doing their best to cheer me up... but it did nothing to lift my spirits.
Was what I had done far worse than I realized?@k ...Had I really done something terrible last night...?
Those thoughts gnawed at me... for the rest of the entire day.
``...See you, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS02/02/130200097. See you tomorrow.``
Before I knew it, I was standing in front of my own house.
``...Mii-chan seemed to have a cold, but... could you be coming down with something too, Keiichi-kun? Could you?``
``Huh...? I'm fine.@k@vS02/01/130100259. Believe it or not, I've never missed school because of a cold.``
``But both you and Mii-chan looked so rough today...``
``...Thanks for worrying about me.@k@vS02/01/130100261. I'll be fine! Idiots don't catch colds, remember?``
Rena smiled, waved goodbye, and walked away.
...Hah.
...I let out a heavy sigh and stepped through the front door.
The moment I got home, Mom called out to me.
``What?``
``Keiichi?@k@vS02/00/132700005. I hate to ask, but could you go to the library and return some books for me?``
``...Ugh, what a pain...!``
``Your father and I are waiting for an important phone call right now, so neither of us can leave the house.@k@vS02/00/132700007. The books are due back today. Could you please take care of it?``
......Good grief.
Was it something to do with Dad's work?@k If they brought up his job, there was no arguing.
``Fine, fine.@k@vS02/01/130100265. ...But I don't even know where the library is.``
``Oh, you don't?@k@vS02/00/132700009. It's just a little past Okinomiya Station...``
Just then, the phone rang.
Both my parents' faces went tense in an instant.
...Mom snatched up the receiver.
``Hello!@k@vS02/00/132700011. This is the Maebara residence. ......Yes?@k@vS02/00/132700012. Yes. He's here.``
The receiver was thrust toward my face.
``It's for you, Keiichi.@k@vS02/00/132700014. Keep it short, all right?``
I was about to ask who it was, but it would be faster to just ask the person on the other end directly.
``Hello?``
``Oh... this is the younger Sonozaki.@k@vS02/06/130600151. Hello.@k@vS02/06/130600152. Is now a good time to talk?``
It was Shion.
...The older sister had been a wreck from staying up all night, but the younger one seemed perfectly fine.
``Shion?@k@vS02/01/130100268. I figured you'd be in bed catching up on sleep, same as Mion.``
``What?@k@vS02/06/130600154. So Sis is playing hooky today?@k@vS02/06/130600155. Ah-hahahahaha! That's so like her~~!``
We shared a warm laugh, but Mom was glaring at me, silently telling me to wrap it up.
``......Oh, whoops... sorry.@k@vS02/01/130100270. My parents are waiting for a call, so I can't talk long.@k@vS02/01/130100271. ...What's up?``
``...There's something I wanted to talk to you about... but if a long call isn't possible, I suppose it can't be helped...``
She sounded a little deflated.
...What could Shion want to discuss badly enough to call me?@k My curiosity was piqued.
``Then how about we meet up in person?@k@vS02/01/130100273. And if you could show me where the library is while you're at it, that'd be a huge help.``
``You mean the municipal library by the station?@k@vS02/06/130600158. Sure, that's fine.@k@vS02/06/130600159. It's on my way to work anyway.``
``Okay, do you know the spot where I usually meet Mion?@k@vS02/01/130100275. That fork in the road where the path to the Sonozaki house branches off—``

``Oh, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600161. Didn't I mention this?@k@vS02/06/130600162. I don't live at the main house—I live in Okinomiya.@k@vS02/06/130600163. So if we could meet by the ticket gates at Okinomiya Station, that would work better for me.``
The main house?@k A house in Okinomiya?
......Come to think of it, I'd always had the sense that the Sonozaki sisters weren't quite in sync... Did they live in different houses?
Now that she mentioned it, Rena had said she'd never once met the younger sister, even when visiting the Sonozaki home to hang out.
``Let's meet at the station, then.@k@vS02/01/130100277. I'll head over right away.@k@vS02/01/130100278. Later!``
The moment I hung up, Mom shoved a thick stack of paperbacks into my hands.
...Six volumes of long mystery novels.@k ...Mom certainly had her hobbies.
``Thank you, Keiichi.@k@vS02/00/132700016. Be home by dinner, okay?``
``Sure thing.@k@vS02/01/130100280. All right, I'm off.``
First things first—change clothes and grab my bike key.
I headed up the stairs to my room.
Shion was already there waiting when I arrived, so meeting up went smoothly.
``Unlike Mion, you actually show up on time...@k@vS02/01/130100282. I'm genuinely impressed.``
``Ahahahaha.@k@vS02/06/130600165. If you use my sister as the standard, you're going to run into all kinds of problems.``
Hm?
Shion seemed a bit unsteady on her feet too. ...She'd clearly been up all night with Mion.
``You didn't get any sleep last night either, did you?@k@vS02/01/130100284. Don't even try to hide it.@k@vS02/01/130100285. And I bet you left school early.``
``Wrong!@k@vS02/06/130600167. I skipped school entirely from the start today.@k@vS02/06/130600168. I'm smarter about these things than Sis.``
Hey now...
I wouldn't exactly call skipping school 'smart'...
``Shall we take care of your errand first?@k@vS02/06/130600170. We don't have a lot of time.@k@vS02/06/130600171. ...You're going to the library, right?``
``Yeah, thanks.@k@vS02/01/130100287. My mom's books are due back today.@k@vS02/01/130100288. ...Is the library far?``
``It's really close.``
Just as Shion said, we spotted the library's signboard almost immediately.
If it was this easy to find, I hadn't really needed Shion to guide me at all...
We turned a corner and the building was right there.
Shishibone Municipal Library.
I hadn't expected much from a rural library... but it was surprisingly large.
The first floor was actually a government office, so only the second floor served as the library, but even so, the collection looked quite impressive.
We passed through the automatic doors and were greeted by the cool blast of an air curtain.
After the cacophony of cicadas outside, the interior was blissfully quiet.
The climate control kept conditions ideal for the books. There was also that faintly musty smell unique to old paper...
``...Every library feels pretty much the same, doesn't it?``
If there was one difference... it was that the seats weren't packed with students cramming for exams.
I used to be one of those students, but that felt like a lifetime ago now.
``Excuse me, I'm here to return these.``
The return process was finished quickly, and they handed back Mom's library card.
Shion was wandering between the bookshelves with a curious look, as if she wasn't the type who visited libraries often.
``...Sorry for the wait.@k@vS02/01/130100292. All done.``
``Libraries are such nice, cool places~@k@vS02/06/130600174. I think I'd like to work at one someday.``
``Not sure if I'd call that modest or practical... It's a pretty humble dream compared to your sister's ambitions.``
Mion would definitely aspire to be some hotshot at an international trading firm, or any other flashy career that had her charging around the globe.
Compared to that... Shion's aspiration was rather endearing.
``Well, we should each cherish what makes us different.@k@vS02/06/130600176. We're not clones—we're two distinct individuals, Shion and Mion.``

``...Being twins gives you a lot to think about, huh?@k@vS02/01/130100295. Still, it sounds fun.@k@vS02/01/130100296. ...I don't have any siblings, so it's just me and my parents at home.@k@vS02/01/130100297. It can feel pretty stifling.``
``It's the same for me, actually.@k@vS02/06/130600178. I don't live with Sis, after all.``
Oh right, she'd mentioned that...@k But why?@k Sisters living apart like that was unusual.
It was tiring to talk while walking, though.
...We ended up finding a deserted lounge area and settling in to chat.
``Ahahahahaha.@k@vS02/06/130600180. That's right.@k@vS02/06/130600181. Sis lives at the main house, and I live in Okinomiya.``
``What do you mean by 'main house'?@k@vS02/01/130100299. ...That sounds awfully grand.``

``The main house is the main house.@k@vS02/06/130600183. The Sonozaki family's main estate.@k@vS02/06/130600184. Since Sis is the heir, she lives with Granny and apparently undergoes all sorts of training to prepare for leading the family.``
Mion... the head of the family?
It somehow fit her and didn't fit her at the same time...
``You just moved here, right, Kei-chan?@k@vS02/06/130600186. ...I know it sounds like bragging, but the Sonozaki family is quite prominent around these parts.``
``Huh? ...Seriously?``
The word 'prominent' didn't match the easygoing, approachable air that Mion and Shion both carried.
According to Shion, the Sonozaki family had been an old established house in Hinamizawa. After the war, they'd succeeded in various business ventures and dramatically expanded their influence.
``The Sonozaki family has a culture where the entire extended family supports one another.@k@vS02/06/130600188. ...It was the current head, Granny, who built it all up.``
The whole family would rally to support any relative's business venture...@k They were like one enormous living corporation.
Navigating tax regulations with ease, one relative after another found business success, fueled by the family's deep coffers.
``If you ever walk around Okinomiya, pay attention to the signboards.@k@vS02/06/130600190. You'd be amazed how many shops are run by members of the Sonozaki family.``
``...Now that you mention it...@k@vS02/01/130100302. That toy shop did seem to be run by a relative.@k@vS02/01/130100303. Does that mean Angel Mort is run by your relatives too?``
...Even the handful of shops I knew about were connected to the Sonozaki family...@k That was genuinely surprising.

``We're especially strong in finance and real estate.@k@vS02/06/130600192. On top of that, a significant portion of the Chamber of Commerce either bears the Sonozaki name or are related to us.@k@vS02/06/130600193. With the Okinomiya area as their voter base, there are even Sonozakis serving on the city council and in the prefectural assembly.``
``......Hold on.@k@vS02/01/130100305. ...This is getting... really intense.``
Relatives running shops across Okinomiya, with particular strength in finance and real estate...?
On top of that, a significant share of the Chamber of Commerce are relatives, and there are even elected officials...
``...Don't tell me...@k@vS02/01/130100307. ......The Sonozaki family is actually... a big deal around here...?``
``Oh, yes.@k@vS02/11/130700002. Quite a big deal.@k@vS02/11/130700003. Nfu-fu-fu-fu!``
Out of nowhere, a heavyset man with a cigarette in his mouth barged into our conversation.
...Wait... I'd seen this guy before......
``Hello there!@k@vS02/11/130700005. Good afternoon.@k@vS02/11/130700006. On a date today, are we?@k@vS02/11/130700007. How enviable.``
``If you thought so, you could have had the decency not to intrude...@k@vS02/06/130600195. Ooishi-san.``
That's right.@k It was Ooishi-san, the police officer.
.....Come to think of it, Mion hadn't seemed to get along with him at all.
``Not at all, my apologies.@k@vS02/11/130700009. I heard a voice I recognized and couldn't help myself.``
Ooishi-san laughed crudely, showing no remorse.
...Shion, watching him, didn't seem to harbor the same level of hostility that Mion had.
``Maebara-san, was it?@k@vS02/11/130700011. You're quite the operator.@k@vS02/11/130700012. Two-timing the Sonozaki sisters, are we?``
``No... That's not... I'm not two-timing anyone...``
I turned to Shion, pleading for backup,@k but she just grinned and offered no help whatsoever.
Shion's gaze drifted.@k ...It landed on the clock.
``Oh, I'm sorry, Kei-chan.@k@vS02/06/130600197. ...I really need to get to work.``
Wait... didn't Shion have something she wanted to talk about...?
...But there was this strange man here now...
``Yeah, another time...@k@vS02/06/130600199. Oh, I'll call you after my shift.@k@vS02/06/130600200. It's okay to call at night, right?``
``Yeah.@k@vS02/01/130100312. Should be fine.``
``Then I'll call you tonight. Talk to you then!@k@vS02/06/130600202. Bye!``
Shion hurried off, practically fleeing the scene.
...Ooishi-san was grinning and waving beside me.
......I had the distinct feeling she'd used me as a decoy to make her escape.@k ...I wasn't even sure she actually had work...
``...Being this close to the Sonozaki daughters makes you quite the VIP, Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700014. Perhaps I should watch my manners around you. Nfu-fu-fu!``
Ooishi-san fed coins into a cup-coffee vending machine while casually saying something outrageous.
``Iced tea okay?@k@vS02/11/130700016. Want milk in it?``
``I'll add some, then.@k@|@y@vS02/11/130700018. Here you go.``
Before I could react, he was handing me a cup of iced tea brimming with ice.
...I couldn't think of any reason to let some random old man buy me a drink, but...@k He'd already gotten it for me, so I thanked him.
Maybe Ooishi-san was just killing time, because despite having barely any connection to me, he launched into all manner of idle chatter.
...When I thought about how I was supposed to be talking with Shion, this felt like a complete disaster...
``How much do you know about Sonozaki-san, Maebara-san?``
``...Nothing beyond what I just heard.``
Until then, I'd known nothing about either Mion or Shion beyond the fact that they were interesting people.
Learning they were daughters of some illustrious family was genuinely news to me.

``The Sonozaki family is a crime syndicate that has this entire region under its thumb.@k@vS02/11/130700021. Incidentally, her father is a high-ranking executive in a syndicate organization that's well known even in the Kanto region.@k@vS02/11/130700022. Were you aware of that?``
``What...?@k@vS02/01/130100317. A high-ranking executive...?``

``You've never been to Shion-san's home?@k@vS02/11/130700024. It's the textbook image of a crime boss's residence—high walls, barbed wire, security cameras.@k@vS02/11/130700025. You should pay her a visit sometime.``
Mion was the heir, she'd said...... but surely not... to a crime syndicate...?

``Oh no, no, no, no, no.@k@vS02/11/130700027. Not at all.@k@vS02/11/130700028. A petty little syndicate isn't the only thing Sonozaki Mion-san stands to inherit.@k@vS02/11/130700029. She's inheriting the Sonozaki family itself.@k@vS02/11/130700030. ......I'll let you chew on what exactly that means.``
Ooishi-san had maintained a teasing tone throughout, but the substance of what he was saying was anything but trivial... it was almost too sudden to believe.

``If Mion-san put her mind to it, she could murder someone in broad daylight in the middle of the street and still make it disappear.@k@vS02/11/130700032. Witnesses wouldn't come forward, and she could apply pressure to the police as well.``
``M-Mion would never kill anyone...!``
``Na-ha-ha-ha... Of course not, of course not. I'm speaking hypothetically.``
``...Um... It was Ooishi-san,@k@vS02/01/130100321. right?``
``Yes, that's right.``

``Ooishi-san.@k@vS02/01/130100323. ...No matter what kind of family Mion comes from, she's the best friend I could ever ask for, and I'm proud of that.@k@vS02/01/130100324. I don't appreciate you speaking about her that way.``
I fixed him with a stare, letting my quiet anger show.
...Of course, with a man this impossible to read, I had no idea whether it got through to him.
``Then let me be direct.@k@vS02/11/130700036. ...If Mion-san were under suspicion for a number of incidents, what would you think?``
Under suspicion?! Incidents?!
There's a limit to how outrageous you can be!
``It has to be a misunderstanding!@k@vS02/01/130100326. Mion might say some rough things, but... she's not the kind of person who would do something like that!!``
``Maebara-san, please keep it down...@k@vS02/11/130700038. We're in a library.``
Ooishi-san shushed me, but my agitation wasn't about to subside so easily.
``...Please, calm down.@k@vS02/11/130700040. As I told you before, the Sonozaki family holds enormous power in this region, for better or for worse.``
``So whenever anything happens, suspicion naturally falls on them.@k@vS02/11/130700042. ...That's what I'd like you to understand.``
......Well... I understood what Ooishi-san was getting at.
Taking Shion's account into consideration as well, the Sonozaki family was a massive clan that controlled everything from Hinamizawa to Okinomiya.
And Mion was its heir, apparently wielding strong influence over the entire family.
But... even so, suspecting Mion of being involved in incidents?!
``Which is why I'm only doing this as part of my job.@k@vS02/11/130700044. Not to cast doubt on Mion-san, but to clear her name... That's how I'd like you to think of it.``
...He certainly had a way with words.
He was the very picture of a cunning, underhanded adult.
By this point, I had begun to realize that this encounter with Ooishi-san was no coincidence... but something deliberate.
``...Ooishi-san, did you come here specifically to talk to me?``
``Yes, I did.@k@vS02/11/130700046. Keiichi Maebara-san.``
...he answered without the slightest hesitation.
``...Mion's under suspicion for something, and you came to question her friend—me.@k@vS02/01/130100329. ...Is that what this is?``

``No, no, that's not it at all.@k@vS02/11/130700048. I didn't come to speak with a friend of Sonozaki Mion-san. I came to speak with Keiichi Maebara-san specifically.@k@vS02/11/130700049. Showing up at your home would have been rather intrusive, I thought.``
``With me......?@k@vS02/01/130100331. What would the police want with me...?``
Realizing that this staged 'coincidence' had been aimed squarely at me, a chill ran down my spine.
``Na-ha-ha-ha.@k@vS02/11/130700051. No need to be so on edge.@k@vS02/11/130700052. Just relax and answer a few questions—that's all.``
``Is this... an interrogation...?``
``Let's just call it a chat with a guy you happened to run into. Nfu-fu-fu-fu!``
Ooishi-san spoke casually, trying to create a relaxed atmosphere.
...But behind his eyes lurked a light that appeared and vanished—sharp as a hawk's gaze.
If I tried to flee, this man might actually haul me in and sit me down in a real interrogation room.@k ......That was an utterly unbearable thought.
``...Last night's Watanagashi Festival was quite the grand event, wasn't it?``
``Don't dodge the question, please...!@k@vS02/01/130100334. I can't make small talk right now...``
``The night of the Watanagashi.``
``...Did you see Jirou Tomitake-san and Miyo Takano-san?``
......My stomach dropped.
I could feel my body flinch involuntarily.
``Hmm... where did I put those photos?@k@vS02/11/130700058. ...Ah.@k@vS02/11/130700059. There they are.``
He opened a small notebook and pulled out two photographs.@k ...I didn't even need to look.
``I... Well...@k@vS02/01/130100336. I'm not sure...@k@vS02/01/130100337. I might have run into them, I can't really say...``
This was the second time today I'd been asked this question.@k ...And for the second time, I gave the same answer.
``Oh? Your memory's that hazy?@k@vS02/11/130700061. Don't tell me you were drinking and can't remember. Nfu-fu-fu-fu!``
This, too, was the same as when Mion had asked.@k ...I could tell he wasn't pleased with my vague response...
Why... was he asking me... the same things Mion had...?
The dark mood I had nearly forgotten thanks to my chat with Shion came rushing back all at once.
......Sneaking into the sacred ritual storehouse—a sacrilegious act.
...It was just a momentary impulse... I know it's too late, but... I do regret it...
``Let me ask you once more.@k@vS02/11/130700063. ...At last night's festival, did you see Jirou Tomitake-san and Miyo Takano-san?``
His tone remained gentle... but there was an unmistakable intensity now, clearly different from before.@k ......I was scared.@k That was exactly what I felt...
``...Er... Even if you keep asking... m-my answer is the same as before...``
I don't really remember.
...No answer could be more conveniently evasive.
...I didn't have the courage to lie and say I hadn't seen them... nor the courage to admit that I had...
``I see.@k@vS02/11/130700065. ...I just thought that if I asked again, I might get a different answer.``
I was too frightened... to even meet Ooishi-san's eyes.
``Let me change the question.``
``...Come now, Maebara-san.@k@vS02/11/130700068. Relax, relax.``
Ooishi-san grabbed my shoulders and squeezed, as if giving me a massage.@k ...It just hurt.
Still...... why... would he ask the exact same things as Mion?
...If his questions mirrored Mion's exactly, then the next one had to be...
``On the same night of the festival, did you happen to see Sonozaki Shion-san?``
......Clatter, clatter, clatter.
...I wondered what that sound was. Looking down, I saw it was the unmelted ice in the cup I was gripping—rattling.
...My trembling hands were shaking the ice, making it clink.
...Noticing that, I hastily dumped the remaining ice into my mouth and crunched down on it.
``......S... Sorry...@k@vS02/01/130100340. I might have seen her... but, well... she looks just like Mion, so...@k@vS02/01/130100341. ......I could've just been mixing them up...``
``Na-ha-ha-ha.@k@vS02/11/130700071. ...Now, that can't be right.``
Suddenly, Ooishi-san gripped both my shoulders firmly and bent down so our eyes were level.
...So this is what it feels like... to be a mouse stared down by a snake.
I wanted to look away... but I couldn't...
A piercing gaze that pinned you in place... I was experiencing it for the first time in my life...
``Mion-san and Shion-san dress completely differently.@k@vS02/11/130700073. You wouldn't mix them up that easily, would you?@k@vS02/11/130700074. Maebara-san.``
His tone was harsh.@k ......Harsh—just like when Mion had pressed me.
I could feel my heart pounding faster.
...When I realized he could feel it through his hands on my shoulders, the fear only deepened.
...Why... why were both Mion and Ooishi-san... asking me about last night...?
Was sneaking into the storehouse really that...... bad...... urgh.........
...I never should have let Takano-san talk me into it.
Tomitake-san casually said 'go ahead and check it out,' so... I just...
......And if Shion... hadn't encouraged me either......
No... I knew.
The one most at fault was myself.
......If only I hadn't given in to that foolish curiosity and entered the forbidden storehouse......
``Ooishi-san.@k@vS02/19/133100002. They're playing the closing music.``
A young man in a suit entered the lounge.
...From his bearing, I could tell immediately he was Ooishi-san's subordinate.
``Hm? Oh, so it is.@k@vS02/11/130700076. Closing time already?``
I hadn't noticed until he said it.
...At some point, the closing announcement and music had started playing without my awareness.
...I had been so agitated, so overwhelmed, that I hadn't even heard it.
``Well then, Maebara-kun.@k@vS02/11/130700078. It's gotten late, so we'll take our leave.@k@vS02/11/130700079. Let's continue this conversation sometime when we have more time—at your leisure.``
...No thanks.@k I never wanted to see him again...
Ooishi-san started to leave the lounge with his subordinate.@k ......But just as I thought he was gone, he spun back around.
``Oh, right, Maebara-san.``
``About those two questions I asked you.@k@vS02/11/130700082. ...Your answer to both was 'I don't really remember,' correct?``

``For your reference.@k@vS02/11/130700084. ...You did see all of them yesterday.@k@vS02/11/130700085. The four of you were walking together, looking like you were having a wonderful time.@k@vS02/11/130700086. ......Near the stone steps.@k@vS02/11/130700087. I saw it with my own eyes.``
...My breath caught in my throat and my heart nearly stopped.
......What a vile man.
...He... knew all along... and he was testing me...
``Until we meet again.@k@vS02/11/130700089. Take care now.``
I sat frozen like stone, unable to move... until the clacking of his leather shoes had completely faded into silence...
